title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: Stream metabolism models estimate gross primary production ecosystem respiration continuous sensor records. streamMetabolizer package fits models, requires specific set input variables: vignette walks computing inputs french_creek dataset using preMetabolizer. data come field study French Creek near Laramie, Wyoming, USA (Hall et al. 2016, https://doi.org/10.1890/14-0631.1). keep NASA-derived PAR light.obs modeled PAR calc_light() light.calc two light series can compared.

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: dataset covers 15-minute intervals August 23 September 30, 2012. Two sensors deployed site: "REZN" ran start study September 1 12:50 MDT, replaced "TOWN". plot shows full record sensors.  REZN sensor shows cluster anomalous negative temperature values near end deployment. rest vignette filter TOWN sensor, longer cleaner record.
code: data(french_creek) french_creek <- french_creek |>    filter(lubridate::minute(datetime) %in% c(0, 15, 30, 45))  glimpse(french_creek) #> Rows: 3,629 #> Columns: 4 #> $ datetime <dttm> 2012-08-23 23:15:00, 2012-08-23 23:30:00, 2012-08-23 23:45:0… #> $ sonde    <chr> "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN… #> $ temp_C   <dbl> 14.13, 13.86, 13.60, 13.36, 13.19, 13.02, 12.88, 12.76, 12.61… #> $ DO_mgL   <dbl> 7.40, 7.30, 7.27, 7.25, 7.26, 7.28, 7.25, 7.23, 7.12, 7.03, 6… french_creek |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(temp_C, DO_mgL),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(variable = recode(     variable,     temp_C = "Temperature (°C)",     DO_mgL = "DO (mg/L)"   )) |>   ggplot(aes(datetime, value, color = sonde)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   scale_color_manual(values = c(REZN = "#E69F00", TOWN = "#0072B2")) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, color = "Sensor") +   theme_bw() #> Warning: Removed 272 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). french_creek <- french_creek |>   filter(sonde == "TOWN") |>   select(-sonde)

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: Negative water temperatures physically impossible liquid water indicate sensor malfunction. values <= 5 mg/L improbable may indicate sensor malfunction. ’ll remove rows.
code: french_creek <- french_creek |>   filter(is.na(temp_C) | temp_C >= 0) |>    filter(is.na(DO_mgL) | DO_mgL > 5)

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: streamMetabolizer requires evenly spaced data. even_timesteps() fills gaps NA rows interval consistent.
code: french_creek <- even_timesteps(french_creek, datetime_col = "datetime")

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: streamMetabolizer works mean solar time, shifts UTC longitude-based offset noon falls roughly sun highest. define site constants reuse throughout.
code: lat <- 41.33 lon <- -106.3  french_creek <- french_creek |>   mutate(     solar.time = convert_UTC_to_solartime(       datetime,       longitude = lon,       time.type = "mean solar"     )   ) |>    filter(solar.time >= as.POSIXct("2012-09-18 04:05:58") &          solar.time <= as.POSIXct("2012-09-21 03:50:58"))

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: calc_light() returns photosynthetically active radiation µmol/m²/s based solar geometry site. takes mean solar time input.
code: french_creek <- french_creek |>   mutate(light.calc = calc_light(solar.time, latitude = lat, longitude = lon))

what: Get barometric pressure and observed light
title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: French Creek sits roughly 2,195 m (7,200 ft) sea level. Using standard sea-level pressure (101.325 kPa) overpredict O2 saturation 20%, download NASA POWER surface pressure site get_nasa_data(). also download shortwave irradiance; get_nasa_data() converts observed PAR light.obs interpolates NASA fields timestamps french_creek.
code: french_elev_m <- get_usgs_elev(   latitude = lat,   longitude = lon,   units = "Meters" )  nasa_dat <- get_nasa_data(   data = french_creek,   datetime_col = "datetime",   lat = lat,   lon = lon,   elev_m = french_elev_m,   params = c("PSC", "ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN") ) |>   select(datetime = dateTime, bp_kPa = PSC, light.obs) #>  #> ── Downloading NASA POWER data ───────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Site: single site ── #>  #> ℹ   Retrieving data: 2012-09-18 to 2012-09-21 #> ✔ Finished downloading data for single site  nasa_dat #> # A tibble: 287 × 3 #>    datetime            bp_kPa light.obs #>    <dttm>               <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 2012-09-18 11:15:00   69.4       0   #>  2 2012-09-18 11:30:00   69.4       0   #>  3 2012-09-18 11:45:00   69.4       0   #>  4 2012-09-18 12:00:00   69.4       0   #>  5 2012-09-18 12:15:00   69.4      42.6 #>  6 2012-09-18 12:30:00   69.4      85.1 #>  7 2012-09-18 12:45:00   69.5     128.  #>  8 2012-09-18 13:00:00   69.5     170.  #>  9 2012-09-18 13:15:00   69.5     274.  #> 10 2012-09-18 13:30:00   69.5     379.  #> # ℹ 277 more rows  french_data <- french_creek |>   left_join(nasa_dat, by = "datetime")

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: site-adjusted pressure water temperature, calc_O2sat() returns saturation concentration mg/L.
code: french_data <- french_data |>   mutate(     DO.sat = calc_O2sat(       temp_water = temp_C,       atmo_press = bp_kPa,       units      = "kPa"     )   )  summary(french_data$DO.sat) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   7.211   7.723   8.367   8.257   8.781   9.153

title: Preparing French Creek Data for Stream Metabolism Modeling
text: final step restructures data retains observed calculated light.   fit model streamMetabolizer::metab(), choose either light.obs light.calc rename column light. See streamMetabolizer documentation details model fitting interpreting metabolism estimates.
code: sm_input <- french_data |>   transmute(     solar.time = solar.time,     DO.obs     = DO_mgL,     DO.sat     = DO.sat,     depth      = 0.16,     temp.water = temp_C,     light.obs  = light.obs,     light.calc = light.calc   )  head(sm_input) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   solar.time          DO.obs DO.sat depth temp.water light.obs light.calc #>   <dttm>               <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 2012-09-18 04:10:58   8.4    9.05  0.16       3.58       0            0 #> 2 2012-09-18 04:25:58   8.42   9.06  0.16       3.54       0            0 #> 3 2012-09-18 04:40:58   8.42   9.07  0.16       3.5        0            0 #> 4 2012-09-18 04:55:58   8.42   9.08  0.16       3.46       0            0 #> 5 2012-09-18 05:10:58   8.44   9.09  0.16       3.42      42.6          0 #> 6 2012-09-18 05:25:58   8.47   9.10  0.16       3.37      85.1          0 sm_input |>   mutate(DO.pctsat = 100 * DO.obs / DO.sat) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(DO.obs, DO.sat, DO.pctsat),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(units = ifelse(variable == "DO.pctsat", "DO\n(% sat)", "DO\n(mg/L)")) |>   ggplot(aes(solar.time, value, color = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_grid(units ~ ., scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "Solar time", y = NULL, color = "Variable") +   theme_bw() labels <- c(   depth      = "depth~(m)",   temp.water = "water~temp~(degree*C)",   light.obs  = "atop(observed~PAR, (mu*mol~m^{-2}~s^{-1}))",   light.calc = "atop(calculated~PAR, (mu*mol~m^{-2}~s^{-1}))" )  sm_input |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(depth, temp.water, light.obs, light.calc),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(     variable = ordered(       variable,       levels = c("depth", "temp.water", "light.obs", "light.calc")     )   ) |>   ggplot(aes(solar.time, value, color = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_grid(     variable ~ .,     scales = "free_y",     labeller = ggplot2::as_labeller(labels, label_parsed)   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c(       depth      = "#333333",       temp.water = "#e8000d",       light.obs  = "#f2a900",       light.calc = "#f5c542"     )   ) +   labs(x = "Solar time", y = NULL, color = "Variable") +   theme_bw()

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: Computes suggested bin width histogram using one several classical rules. NA values silently removed computation.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
code: calc_bin_width(x, method = "auto")

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: x Numeric vector observations. Must contain least two distinct, finite, non-NA values. method Single character string specifying binning rule. One : "auto" Minimum Freedman-Diaconis Sturges (default). Tends work well across wide range distributions. "sturges" Sturges' rule: \(h = R / (\log_2 n + 1)\). Assumes approximate normality; tends undersmooth large \(n\). "fd" Freedman-Diaconis: \(h = 2 \cdot \mathrm{IQR}(x)     \cdot n^{-1/3}\). Robust outliers. Falls back Sturges \(\mathrm{IQR}(x) = 0\). "sqrt" Square-root rule: \(h = R / \sqrt{n}\). Simple heuristic used spreadsheet applications. "rice" Rice rule: \(h = R / (2 n^{1/3})\). Similar Sturges grows slowly. "scott" Scott's rule: \(h = 3.49 \hat\sigma n^{-1/3}\). Optimal normal data sense minimising mean integrated squared error. "doane" Doane's rule. Extends Sturges account skewness; better suited non-normal distributions. Requires \(n \ge 3\).

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: single positive numeric value giving bin width.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: formulae, \(R\) denotes range (diff(range(x))) \(n\) number non-NA observations. "fd" fallback Sturges IQR(x) == 0 avoids zero-width bin, can occur heavily discrete zero-inflated data.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: function aborts informative message : x numeric vector. method single character string. x contains non-NA values, one. non-NA values x identical (zero range). method = "doane" requested fewer 3 observations (skewness standard error undefined \(n < 3\)). unrecognised method string supplied.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
code: # Continuous data — Freedman-Diaconis calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "fd") #> [1] 0.448634  # Skewed data — Doane corrects for skewness calc_bin_width(rexp(200), "doane") #> [1] 0.3778829  # Discrete / zero-inflated data — FD falls back to Sturges calc_bin_width(c(rep(0, 50), rep(1, 50)), "fd") #> [1] 0.4308869  # NA values are silently dropped calc_bin_width(c(NA, rnorm(100), NA), "scott") #> [1] 0.7285563  # Compare all methods on the same data x <- rnorm(500) methods <- c("auto", "sturges", "fd", "sqrt", "rice", "scott", "doane") vapply(methods, \(m) calc_bin_width(x, m), numeric(1)) #>      auto   sturges        fd      sqrt      rice     scott     doane  #> 0.3280859 0.5811504 0.3280859 0.2590091 0.3648492 0.4261072 0.5560889   # Error conditions try(calc_bin_width(letters))               # non-numeric x #> Error in calc_bin_width(letters) : `x` must be a numeric vector. #> ✖ Got an object of class <character>. try(calc_bin_width(c(1, NA, NA)))          # only one non-NA value #> Error in calc_bin_width(c(1, NA, NA)) :  #>   `x` contains only one non-"NA" value. #> ℹ At least two observations are required to compute a bin width. try(calc_bin_width(rep(5, 50)))            # zero range #> Error in calc_bin_width(rep(5, 50)) :  #>   `x` has zero range: all non-"NA" values are identical. #> ℹ Bin width is undefined when all observations are the same. try(calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "badrule")) # unknown method #> Error in calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "badrule") :  #>   Invalid `method` argument. #> ✖ Method "badrule" is not supported. #> ℹ Allowed methods: "auto", "sturges", "fd", "sqrt", "rice", "scott", and #>   "doane" try(calc_bin_width(c(1, 2), "doane"))      # doane needs n >= 3 #> Error in calc_bin_width(c(1, 2), "doane") :  #>   Doane's rule requires at least 3 observations. #> ✖ `x` has only 2 non-"NA" values after removing "NA"s. #> ℹ Use a different `method` or supply more data.

title: preMetabolizer 0.0.0.9000
text: convert_PAR_to_SW() convert_SW_to_PAR() longer re-exported preMetabolizer. Call streamMetabolizer::convert_PAR_to_SW() streamMetabolizer::convert_SW_to_PAR() directly (#TBD). Added french_creek dataset: 5-minute dissolved oxygen water temperature records French Creek, Laramie, WY (Aug–Sep 2012), courtesy Bob Hall. Source: Hall et al. (2016), . Added “Preparing French Creek Data Stream Metabolism Modeling” vignette demonstrating complete preMetabolizer workflow: solar time conversion, modeled PAR, elevation-corrected barometric pressure, O2 saturation, streamMetabolizer input assembly. calc_O2sat() now uses Benson Krause umol/kg coefficients Garcia Gordon (1992), converts mg/L salinity-aware density, applies corrected vapor-pressure term non-standard pressure (#TBD). calc_vapor_press() method = "MIMSY" now returns physically correct values. Antoine equation constant B incorrectly set 140.264 (1435.264), causing vapor pressures orders magnitude large. calc_water_height() now gives clear error type = "unvented" used without providing atmo_kPa, rather silently returning NA. even_timesteps() longer errors single-column data frames due drop = TRUE subsetting. get_ks_meso() now correctly reports invalid variable names error message (previously reported “Invalid stations:” instead “Invalid variables:”). get_nasa_data() now accepts time-series data directly, infers per-site download date ranges data, uses either single-site coordinate arguments per-site lat, lon, elev_m columns, interpolates NASA values input timestamps, returns light.obs converting ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN streamMetabolizer::convert_SW_to_PAR() (#TBD). get_usgs_elev() can now retrieve elevation values USGS Elevation Point Query Service one coordinate pairs (#TBD). calc_water_density() longer accepts .drop_units argument; always returns plain numeric vector (#TBD). convert_flow() now requires explicit argument specifying input unit; always returns plain numeric vector instead units object (#TBD). convert_pressure() now requires explicit argument; always returns plain numeric vector. Unit-bearing objects longer accepted input (#TBD). correct_bp() longer accepts drop_units argument; always returns plain numeric vector. Elevation inputs must plain numeric (meters) (#TBD). has_units() removed. units package longer dependency (#TBD).
